user_message: Summarize what happened with Fourth Coffee and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 14:57 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 10:01 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 17:11 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fourth Coffee escalation triage

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Infrastructure Manager is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-03-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Priya (Fourth Coffee)

[00:01:15] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:07:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:15:00] Priya (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-E trap (Misses the most recent thread); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-E failure mode (Misses the most recent thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-E -- Misses the most recent thread
